title: Significance of Wings
wikitext:
[]: Significance of Wings             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:25 pm Ok, so today i was riding in a car, and for something reason, i was thinking about church. I was thinking about Revealtions and how Joseph Smith (yes, im mormon) explained some of the symbolism. One that stuck with me was that the wings of the angels represent the ability to move, think, act, etc. In evangelion, the only 'beings' with wings that we see are: GNR, Unit-01, and Adam What significance/symbolism is to be found? Here are some of my ideas -Used to express lillith's helplessness and her ascent to power. As the progresses during 3I, she grows wings, and they bigger and bigger, perhaps symbolizing her growing sentinence and control. -After 3I is interrupted, if i remember right, GNR's wing 'wilt' (if thats the proper word), symbolizing her loss of controll of the situation -As for Adam, during 2I, her wings spread, symbolizing freedom, but he couldnt handle it, manifesting itself in a massive energy release, resulting in her becoming an embryo (lower energy state) -perhaps, this inability to properly control freedom passed down to her children. The Angels, would be dammed to forever seek their mother, mindlessly, and without deviation this is one of my theories plz comment

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:33 pm eva on also has wings in 3I I think its a power thing. That and they look soooo damn cool

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:38 pm Yeah the sciensists freaked out because Adam extended his wings, it's some kind of visual representation of power and like Datdude said.....they look freakin cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:38 pm Technically, Arael has wings, too. And Pen^2 has ones that have modified into flippers. </pointless> SEELE 08 wrote: it's some kind of visual representation of power

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:39 pm thats what i forgot As soon as Unit-01 grows wings, or shortly before, she sheds her bindings. Perhaps this was a prelude to the immense FREEDOM her DECISION would play in 3I. She had the power to choose to stop Third Impact, and she did. Again symbolizing freedom. Is it relevant that although they are called Angels, they have no wings (excluding Adam and Lilith)? Perhaps Anno was emphasizing their lack of will/ free agency

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:40 pm Duh Pen=Pen is god. Everyone knows that. The angels only attack after he's been mistreated.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:42 pm Reichu wrote: Technically, Arael has wings, too. And Pen^2 has ones that have modified into flippers. </pointless> SEELE 08 wrote: it's some kind of visual representation of power

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:50 pm InvaderRen wrote: HEY, im the one promoting it...

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Sat Jul 15, 2006 10:56 pm eh, in my theory, i meant that the wings mean the ability to act, think, speak, choose etc, not only power "extreme manisfestation of power" was not the idea i was trying to convey although, i do like your theory... im off for tonight

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 15, 2006 11:03 pm InvaderRen wrote: eh, in my theory, i meant that the wings mean the ability to act, think, speak, choose etc, not only power "extreme manisfestation of power" was not the idea i was trying to convey

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 16, 2006 2:26 am Lesse... twelve... Twelve is the number of The twelve original disciples of Jesus. Girls are also considered women in Judaism upon the Age of 12, wherehas the men are considered mature upon the age of 13. Most I can think of, though. Maturety means you're responsible for your own acts, no longer under your parents complete protection, in a way cut from the umblical cord . The twelve wings can mean freedom, and if you consider the Black Moon Lilith's womb, it can symbolize rebirth, showing that Shinji and Shogouki are no longer dependent upon their mother. This goes pretty deep-Firstly, Shogouki is Lilith's 'child'. Secondly, Shinji is the only un-Instrumentalised human (at that point, at least), and humans came from Lilith, who was their 'mother'. And then there's Shinji bidding his mother goodbye, just moments later, being capable of accepting life without his dear mama.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 16, 2006 2:37 am When I talk about 12 wings, I'm thinking more in terms of this guy: Twelve is the number of wings Sammael (= Satan) is said to possess. That's not just doing raw numerology (grabbing at anything "12"), it's "12 Wings ", thus providing a solid point of reference.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 16, 2006 2:41 am Reichu wrote: When I talk about 12 wings, I'm thinking more in terms of this guy: Twelve is the number of wings Sammael (= Satan) is said to possess. That's not just doing raw numerology (grabbing at anything "12"), it's "12 Wings ", thus providing a solid point of reference.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 16, 2006 5:01 am Legend of the Past wrote: And I don't need to tell you Sho-Chan is reffered to as Satan.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 16, 2006 5:25 am Well, Sammael has twelve wings and an Angel mask. One could say EVAs are Angels, plus he has eyes on his wings, another trait shared by 01. I dunno, maybe it's a power any EVA\Angel can achieve, Unit 01 was just lucky enough to achieve it?

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Sun Jul 16, 2006 3:00 pm Reichu wrote: When I talk about 12 wings, I'm thinking more in terms of this guy: Twelve is the number of wings Sammael (= Satan) is said to possess. That's not just doing raw numerology (grabbing at anything "12"), it's "12 Wings ", thus providing a solid point of reference.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 16, 2006 9:49 pm InvaderRen wrote: What is that exactly? When i first saw it, I labled it some random Seraph. As the series progressed, i concluded that it was Adam ~or~ the Seraph, Can somebody clue me in on this whole Sammael thing?

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Tue Jul 18, 2006 9:49 pm So decided to go back to read the passage and discovered something that may shed some light on that figure. Q. What are we to understand by the eyes and wings, which the beasts had? A. Their eyes are a representation of light and knowledge, that is, they are full of knowledge; and their wings are a representation of power, to move, to act, etc. (Doctrine and Covenants 77:4) From there, I looked up the verses they were reffering to. I will include the adjacent verse for reasons you will see... 6 And before the thone there was a sea of glass like unto crystal: and in the midst of the throne, and round about the throne, were four beasts full of eyes before and behind. 7 And the first beast was like a lion, and the second beast like a calf, and the third beast had a face as a man, and the fourth beast ws like a flying eagle. (Revelations 4:6-7) Now to analyize this in terms of Evangelion. The figure shown in the theme, Ill call him the Seraph, is shown having 12 wings and an unknown amount of eyes. According to the first passage, the eyes symbolize knowledge and the wings, power; perhaps symboling the fruits of life and knowlegde. Now, who is the only character in the series with both knowledge and the power to act upon that (combination of the fruit of knowledge and life)? Evangelion Unit 01 As for the second passage, we must anaylize both the pilot and the piloted. Evangelion Unit 01 can be seen: roaring aggresively, as to threaten or attack (Lion) on all fours, 'grazing' on an Angel (Cattle) spreading his wings and ascending into the air (Eagle) face of Eva Unit 01 vaguely resembles a human [they are human after all] (Human) My theory is that Shogouki is the 'beast' before the throne. Now who is ON the throne? "And before the thone there was a sea of glass like unto crystal" Sea of Glass: clear, reflective, seemingly empty, great motion may be underneath. Shinji, our anti-hero, on the Throne of Souls. So according to this theory: Evangelion 01 the the being represented in the picture Evangelion Unit 01 is the combination of the four beasts and Shinji is the the one, literally, on the Throne of the Soul so what do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Wed Jul 19, 2006 12:14 am sweet. makes sense at least i see Eva 01's wings as a proof of it's -angelic-, -celestial- and god-like existance

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Wed Jul 19, 2006 1:32 am But then are you saying Eva-01 is Satan?
